zh.Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Forster於大約1368年出生在英國諾圖波藍德班包。他和Elizabeth De Buckton至少有1個女兒。他去世在英國史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴。

zh-hans.Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Forster于大约1368年出生在英国諾圖波藍德班包。他和Elizabeth De Buckton至少有1个女儿。他去世在英国史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴。

nameMeanings.name: Forster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

variants.name: Foster
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Forrest
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper or worker in one. Middle English forest was not, as today, a near-synonym of wood, but referred specifically to a large area of woodland reserved by law for the purposes of hunting by the king and his nobles. The same applied to the European cognates, both ancient Germanic and Romance. The English word is from Middle English forest ‘forest’, Old French forest, Late Latin forestis (silva). This is generally taken to be a derivative of foris ‘outside’; the reference was probably to woods lying outside a settlement. On the other hand, Middle High German for(e)st has been held to be a derivative of Old High German foraha ‘fir’ (see Forster ), with the addition of a collective suffix.
Irish: this name is also frequently attested in Ireland, where it may be a variant of   Forrestal  .

variants.name: Forrester
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English forester ‘officer in charge of a forest or lord's hunting ground’ (Old French forestier, Anglo-Norman forester). See Forrest .

variants.name: Fuster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Armando, Blanca, Ricardo, Carlos, Pedro, Vicente, Alfredo, Ana, Angelina, Barbaro.
Catalan: occupational name from fuster ‘carpenter’.
German and Jewish: variant of   Forster   3 and 4.

variants.name: Foerst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Klaus.
German (Först): shortened form of Förster or Förstner (see Forster 3). Compare Forst .

variants.name: Forsmann
meanings: North German: variant of Forstmann, itself a variant of Förster (see Forster ).

variants.name: Forstner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Gerhard, Erna, Heinz, Ignaz, Ilse, Monika.
German (also Förstner): variant of Forster .
Slovenian (also Forštner): topographic or occupational name for someone who lived or worked in a forest, of German origin (see above and Forster ).

variants.name: Ferster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: variant of Förster (see Forster 3).

variants.name: Foerster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerda, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Claus, Erwin, Alois, Bernd, Christoph, Detlef, Erna, Gerd.
German (Förster): see Forster .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Forster was born about 1368, in Bamburgh, Northumberland, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Elizabeth De Buckton. He died in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Forster est né environ 1368, à Bamburgh, Northumberland, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Elizabeth De Buckton. Il est décédé à Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Forster wurde um 1368 in Bamburgh, Northumberland, England geboren. Er hatte mit Elizabeth De Buckton mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Forster è nato il circa 1368 nel Bamburgh, Northumberland, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Elizabeth De Buckton. Morì nel Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England.

ja.Bamburgh, Northumberland, England: イギリスノースウンバーランドバンブルグ
ja.Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England: イギリスシュロプシュアイアカストレ-プウルバーバトチ
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Forsterは約1368年にイギリスノースウンバーランドバンブルグで生まれた。彼はElizabeth De Bucktonとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼はイギリスシュロプシュアイアカストレ-プウルバーバトチで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Forster(은)는 대략 1368년 영국 노르스벌랜드 바버그h에서 태어났다. 그는 Elizabeth De Buckton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 슈럽실르 카스틀레-풀버바트치에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Forster nasceu aproximadamente 1368, em Bamburgh, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Elizabeth De Buckton. Ele faleceu em Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Forster родился около 1368 г. в местности бамбург, нортумбирланд, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Elizabeth De Buckton. Он умер в местности кастле-пулвирбач, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Forster nació aproximadamente 1368, en Bamburgh, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Elizabeth De Buckton. Murió en Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
